opportunities.title: Confirmed: Russia Hacked Poland's Power Grid — And the EU Just Said So Out Loud
opportunities.why_now: EU and UK issued a rare joint official attribution of the Polish grid attack to Russian state hackers, turning a private report into diplomatic fact.
opportunities.angle: Breach-digest lead item: timeline of the attack, what changes now that it's officially attributed (sanctions, NATO Article 5 chatter), and why grid operators everywhere should treat this as the new baseline threat model.

opportunities.title: Progress Just Killed ShareFile Servers Overnight — Nobody Will Say Why
opportunities.why_now: An emergency, unexplained shutdown order for a widely-deployed enterprise file transfer product is exactly the kind of unresolved cliffhanger that drives return visits.
opportunities.angle: 'Developing' style post: what we know, what Progress isn't saying, comparisons to past MOVEit/Citrix Bleed-style disasters, and a checklist for admins to isolate exposed instances now.

opportunities.title: Perfect 10s, Actively Exploited: CISA Flags Joomla Extension RCEs Right Now
opportunities.why_now: CISA's KEV addition plus active exploitation of maximum-severity Joomla bugs means unpatched sites are being popped today, not hypothetically.
opportunities.angle: Patch-cadence urgency post: list affected extensions/CVEs, exploitation indicators, and a blunt 'patch now or get owned' framing with a countdown-style hook.

opportunities.title: npm's Bad Week: A Backdoored Jscrambler Package and 148 Fake 'Student Proxy' Libraries Turned Browsers Into a Botnet
opportunities.why_now: Two separate but simultaneous npm supply-chain compromises in the same 48 hours show the ecosystem is under sustained assault, a classic 'inevitable breach' twofer.
opportunities.angle: Supply-chain roundup: how each attack slipped past review, what packages/versions to purge immediately, and a broader 'can you trust npm right now' gut-check for dev teams.

opportunities.title: Microsoft Just Mapped a Full Year of ShinyHunters' Salesforce Playbook — Here's Every Path In
opportunities.why_now: Microsoft's threat intel team publishing three distinct attack paths tied to a year-long ShinyHunters campaign against Salesforce orgs is a rare gift of actionable breach forensics.
opportunities.angle: Deep-dive breakdown of each attack path with detection/mitigation notes, framed as 'if you run Salesforce, check these three things today.'
